channel.title: cbtcbook.com — Articles on Communications-Based Train Control
channel.description: Technical articles on CBTC procurement, agency case studies, standards, and operations. Drawn from twenty years of US deployment experience.

item.title: AI / ML in CBTC: Real Use Cases vs Hype (2026)
item.description: An honest 2026 assessment of where artificial intelligence and machine learning actually deliver value in Communications-Based Train Control, where the deployments are real, and where the marketing has run ahead of the engineering.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: Inside the CBTC Braking Curve: How Safe Stopping Distance Is Calculated
item.description: The kinematic physics, gradient and adhesion mechanics, and reaction-time budgets that turn v² = u² + 2as into the Emergency Braking Curve every Communications-Based Train Control train obeys 20 times a second.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: How to Write a CBTC RFP That Doesn't Lock You Into One Vendor
item.description: A practical framework for US transit agencies drafting a Communications-Based Train Control procurement that preserves competition for the next 30 years. Drawn from twenty years of US deployment experience.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: CBTC Scope of Work: A Template for US Transit Agencies
item.description: A working CBTC scope-of-work outline for US transit agencies, with the section structure, deliverables, and specifying language an FTA-fundable procurement actually needs.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: CBTC vs Traditional Signaling: 8 Concrete Differences
item.description: An engineer's side-by-side of CBTC vs traditional fixed-block signaling: separation, capacity, safety, infrastructure, headway, cost, degraded mode, and operations.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: GoA 0–4 Explained: The Five Grades of Train Automation
item.description: GoA 0 through GoA 4 is the international framework that defines who does what in train operation. This post explains each level, the responsibility allocation, the safety implications, and where US transit deployments actually sit.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: How to Become a CBTC Engineer: A 2026 Career Path
item.description: A 2026 career path for becoming a CBTC engineer in the United States. The educational background that opens the door, the first jobs that build the resume, and the career arc from junior signal engineer to systems integrator and beyond.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: IEEE 1474.1 Explained: Why It's Performance-Based, Not Prescriptive
item.description: IEEE 1474.1 is the cornerstone US CBTC standard. It mandates what a CBTC system must do — headway, safety, availability, response time — and leaves how to suppliers. This post explains the intent, the implications, and the procurement traps.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: London Underground 4LM: The Largest CBTC Retrofit in the World
item.description: A practitioner read of Transport for London's Four Lines Modernisation Programme — the multi-decade Communications-Based Train Control retrofit covering the Circle, District, Hammersmith & City, and Metropolitan lines, awarded to Thales in 2014. What this means for US transit agencies retrofitting century-old infrastructure.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: NYC MTA L Line CBTC: 25 Years of Lessons (2001–2026)
item.description: A practitioner's read of the L Line CBTC project from the 1997 RFP through 2026 operations: Siemens Trainguard MT, the 2006 partial revenue, the 2009 full transition, Hurricane Sandy, and what the rest of US transit owes to it.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: Thales SelTrac vs Hitachi (Ansaldo) ATC: Procurement Implications
item.description: How the 2024 Hitachi Rail acquisition of Thales Ground Transportation Systems collapsed SelTrac and the legacy Ansaldo ATC product into one vendor portfolio, and what that means for US transit procurement decisions.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: Siemens Trainguard MT vs Alstom Urbalis: Architecture Compared
item.description: A neutral architectural comparison of Siemens Trainguard MT and Alstom Urbalis CBTC platforms, oriented to US transit agency procurement decisions.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: What Is Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC)? A 2026 Plain-English Guide
item.description: Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC) explained for US transit professionals: what it is, how it works, why agencies adopt it, and where it stands in 2026.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang
